title: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire
summary: President Donald Trump said that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to make their ceasefire longer by three weeks. A ceasefire is when countries stop fighting. The meeting happened at the White House in Washington, D.C. The Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors met with President Trump. They talked about stopping the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, a group in Lebanon. The first ceasefire was for 10 days and was going to end soon. Now it will last longer. President Trump said the United States will help Lebanon protect itself from Hezbollah. The ambassadors thanked President Trump for his help. They hope to make a lasting peace between Israel and Lebanon. The leaders also want to meet again soon to talk more about peace.

keywords.explanation: An agreement to stop fighting for a period of time.
A person sent by one country to represent it in another country.
A group in Lebanon that has been fighting with Israel.

questions.question: What did President Trump say Israel and Lebanon agreed to do?
How long will the ceasefire be extended?
Who did President Trump meet with?
options: The presidents of Israel and Lebanon
The ambassadors of Israel and Lebanon
The kings of Israel and Lebanon
The generals of Israel and Lebanon
questions.correct_answer: The ambassadors of Israel and Lebanon

questions.question: What group does the United States want to help Lebanon protect itself from?
What did the Lebanese ambassador say to President Trump?
options: She thanked him for his help
questions.correct_answer: She thanked him for his help

background_read: A ceasefire is when two sides agree to stop fighting for a while. This can help them talk about peace. Israel and a group called Hezbollah in Lebanon have been fighting for a long time. The United States often helps countries talk to each other to stop wars. President Trump met with ambassadors from Israel and Lebanon to help them agree to stop fighting.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump, Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors, and other U.S. officials.
WHAT: They agreed to extend the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks.
WHERE: The meeting took place at the White House in Washington, D.C.
WHY: To stop fighting and work toward a lasting peace between Israel and Lebanon.

perspectives.description: Israel wants to stop Hezbollah attacks and protect its people.
Lebanon wants to end Israeli attacks and rebuild its country.
